channel.title: The Grant Desk - next 90 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: next 90 days.

item.title: British Council 90th Anniversary Research Fellowships at IASH 2027 (Edinburgh)
item.description:
Organisation: Institute for Advanced Studies in the Humanities (IASH), University of Edinburgh, in partnership with the British Council

Location: Edinburgh, UK (10 months residential at IASH; final 2 months in home country)

Award: GBP 2,500 per month bursary for 12 months, plus travel expenses, dedicated office space, University of Edinburgh email and library access, a University mentor, weekly Fellows' Lunch, work-in-progress seminars, full calendar of Institute and College events, and opportunities to participate in or design funded workshops and colloquia. No application fee.

Duration: 12 months (10 months residential at IASH, January to October 2027; followed by November-December 2027 knowledge-exchange in the home country)

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Twelve-month postdoctoral fellowship from the Institute for Advanced Studies in the Humanities (IASH) at the University of Edinburgh, in partnership with the British Council, marking the Council's 90th anniversary. Up to two fellowships per year as part of the 2025-2027 partnership. Fellows spend ten months residential at IASH (January to October 2027) followed by up to two months of knowledge-exchange and dissemination work in their home country in collaboration with the British Council. ELIGIBILITY: postdoctoral researchers based in an ODA-recipient country where the British Council operates (full list of ~60+ countries published on the IASH page, spanning Albania to Zimbabwe and including the Occupied Palestinian Territories); PhD completed within the last seven years (career breaks excluded from the seven-year window); applicants must not hold a permanent university position and must not have held a prior IASH Fellowship. Research themes should align with British Council priorities across Arts, Education and English language, plus cross-cutting interests in international relations, soft power, international development, peace building, and cultural relations and diplomacy. NOTE: applicants are required to contact relevant University of Edinburgh researchers before submitting; informational webinar 26 May 2026. Decisions communicated late September 2026. References (minimum two, maximum three) must be emailed by referees directly to iash@ed.ac.uk by the deadline.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Tractor Beam: Issue 6 'The Water Issue' - Call for Submissions
item.description:
Organisation: Tractor Beam

Location: Online publication (remote submissions; geographic eligibility not stated)

Award: $1,000 USD per accepted submission. Accepted work is considered for publication online (Tractor Beam website and Substack) and, in some cases, for one-off printed editions. Stories may also be considered for the Protopian Prize with contributor consent. No submission fee mentioned.

Duration: One-off published piece (no residency component)

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Tractor Beam's sixth issue, themed around water in soil, growth, land and ecosystems large and small, is open for submissions. Editors are seeking anti-apocalyptic visions that explore the future of water in farming and food production, island ecologies, hybrid sea-soil technologies, the people who move water and the people water moves, plus stories about drought, diaspora and what gets carried downstream. FORMAT: stories under 6,000 words; comics 12-16 panels. Submissions accepted via the call page on Tractor Beam's Substack.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Diriyah Art Futures: Emerging New Media Artists Program 2026/27 (Riyadh)
item.description:
Organisation: Diriyah Art Futures (DAF), in collaboration with Le Fresnoy - Studio National des Arts Contemporains

Location: Diriyah, Saudi Arabia (in-person, intensive on-site programme)

Award: Production budget; access to advanced audio-visual labs, immersive-reality and spatial audio-visual environments; cutting-edge professional equipment; multidisciplinary mentorship from international digital and new-media artists; and a culminating curated group exhibition at DAF in October 2027. Specific stipend amount not stated in the public call. No application fee.

Duration: Approximately one year (programme starts October 2026), with three months of thematic and conceptual seminars followed by a practice-based phase and a final group exhibition in October 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Third cycle of Diriyah Art Futures' Emerging New Media Artists Program, a developmental collaboration with Le Fresnoy - Studio National des Arts Contemporains. The first three months centre on thematic and conceptual exploration through seminars, lectures, screenings and workshops spanning digital technology, socio-cultural dynamics, the environment, more-than-human life and cultural heritage. The programme then moves into a practice-based phase with personalised advisory and access to DAF's labs, encompassing advanced audio-visual techniques, immersive reality and spatial audio-visual environments, concluding with a curated group exhibition at DAF in October 2027. ELIGIBILITY: emerging creative practitioners under 35, at graduate or postgraduate level, with experience in modern media and digital art; open worldwide with a particular focus on practitioners from the Middle East and North Africa. Apply via the linked Ministry of Culture engagement portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CBPS Research Fellowship 2026-27 (Bengaluru)
item.description:
Organisation: Centre for Budget and Policy Studies (CBPS)

Location: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India (in-person at CBPS office)

Award: INR 25,000 per month stipend, plus access to office space, internet and the CBPS library. The fellowship does NOT cover housing, transportation or any other financial support. No application fee.

Duration: One year (1 August 2026 to 31 July 2027)

Deadline: 31 May 2026

One-year research fellowship at the Centre for Budget and Policy Studies (CBPS), a non-partisan public policy research institution in Bengaluru, mentoring early-career researchers across CBPS's focus areas: education, gender, governance, health and nutrition, public finance, livelihoods and social security. Fellows work alongside CBPS research staff in a collegial academic environment with in-house talks, seminars, workshops and public events, joining ongoing research projects with mentor guidance. ELIGIBILITY: primarily geared toward scholars who have recently completed their Bachelor's or are in the final year of an undergraduate programme; applications especially encouraged from historically underrepresented groups (class, caste, gender, religion, linguistic affiliation, first-generation scholars); scholars from various disciplines and methodological approaches welcome. Apply by emailing a Statement of Purpose (max 1,500 words), CV/resume in MS Word format, and two letters of reference to fellows@cbps.in. Fellowship awards announced 3 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Grove and Pines Film Festival: Erin Carine Grant for Queer Creatives 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Grove and Pines Film Festival (GAPFF)

Location: United States (festival is US-based; geographic eligibility not specified on the grant page)

Award: $5,000 USD grant awarded annually at the festival. No application fee mentioned.

Duration: One-off project grant

Deadline: 10 Jun 2026

Annual grant from the Grove and Pines Film Festival honouring the legacy of Erin Carine, awarded to a filmmaker whose project embodies creativity, courage and a deep commitment to authentic queer storytelling. ELIGIBILITY: open to individual filmmakers; applicants do not need to have a finished film. The public grant page does not state geographic restrictions, age limits or career-stage requirements - confirm on the linked page before applying. Apply via the Google Form linked from the festival's grant-info page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Medialab Matadero: Collaborative Prototyping Lab - LAB 5 'The Mechanics of Truth' (Madrid)
item.description:
Organisation: Medialab Matadero

Location: Madrid, Spain (in-person, intensive two-week residency)

Award: Travel, accommodation and meals covered for participants residing outside the Community of Madrid; access to Medialab Matadero's prototyping facilities; public presentation of developed projects at the OpenLAB festival. Free to participate. No application fee.

Duration: Two-week residency (12-25 November 2026) followed by public presentation at the OpenLAB festival (26-28 November 2026)

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

Fifth cycle of Medialab Matadero's Collaborative Prototyping Lab, dedicated to exploring how truth is constructed, mediated and contested in contemporary society. The programme supports a wide spectrum of approaches, from highly technical to deeply speculative, and cultivates critical, collaborative prototyping within Madrid's contemporary landscape. Projects developed during the residency are presented publicly as part of the OpenLAB festival immediately afterwards. ELIGIBILITY: practitioners worldwide, aged 18 and older, across visual cultures, technology, architecture, journalism, activism, science and beyond; both project leads and collaborators welcome to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Tokyo Arts and Space (TOKAS): International Creator Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Tokyo Arts and Space (TOKAS)

Location: Tokyo, Japan (in-person; private accommodation plus shared studio)

Award: International airfare; living stipend; a fee for creative work/project; exclusive private accommodation; and access to a shared studio. Specific cash amounts not stated in the public call. No application fee.

Duration: Three-month residency in 2027, concluding with an Open Studio research presentation

Deadline: 16 Jun 2026

Tokyo Arts and Space (TOKAS) International Creator Residency invites practitioners to develop new works and ideas in Tokyo, with research outcomes presented at a closing Open Studio. The programme is aimed at fostering cross-cultural creative exchange across visual arts, design and architecture. ELIGIBILITY: international creators working in visual arts, design or architecture, with at least five years of professional experience in their field. Two letters of recommendation and a portfolio are required. Apply via the online form linked from the TOKAS application package.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Light Work Artist-in-Residence 2027 (Syracuse, NY)
item.description:
Organisation: Light Work

Location: Syracuse, New York, USA (in-person, one-month residency on site)

Award: $7,500 stipend plus a furnished apartment and access to state-of-the-art photography facilities. The residency concludes with a feature in a special edition of 'Contact Sheet: The Light Work Annual' alongside a commissioned essay. No application fee.

Duration: One-month residency in 2027

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

Light Work, an independent non-profit founded in 1973, offers a long-running artist-in-residence programme dedicated to the development of experimental and contemporary photographic practices. Over 400 artists have participated in the residency, with many going on to gain international recognition. ELIGIBILITY: artists working in photography or image-based media, from any country and at any career stage. Apply via the Light Work SlideRoom portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AEIP: Art4Change Storytelling Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Africa-Europe Innovation Partnership (AEIP), implemented by The African Youth Café (TYC), funded by the European Union

Location: Hybrid (online training plus field engagement; applicants based in Africa, Europe or the diaspora)

Award: EUR 3,000 stipend per fellow to cover content production costs (transport, data, basic fieldwork needs), plus structured digital-storytelling training, editorial mentorship and platform access for publishing work. No application fee.

Duration: Six months (June to December 2026)

Deadline: 29 May 2026

Hybrid digital storytelling fellowship from the Africa-Europe Innovation Partnership (AEIP), implemented by The African Youth Café and funded by the European Union, supporting young creatives to document and share stories of youth innovation across Africa, Europe and the diaspora through writing, photography, video, audio and digital storytelling. ELIGIBILITY: applicants aged 18-35 with an interest in storytelling, media or digital content creation, based in Africa, Europe or the diaspora, and able to commit to the full programme. IMPORTANT: as of May 2026 the African applicant quota has been reached - only European applicants are currently being accepted. FORMAT: online training sessions combined with field engagement for content production, plus continuous editorial mentorship and platform access for publishing. The canonical AEIP page does not list a fixed stipend amount, so confirm on application; the Terra Viva summary cites EUR 3,000. Apply via the Microsoft Forms application linked from the AEIP call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BC Arts Council: Individual Arts Grants - Media Artists 2026
item.description:
Organisation: BC Arts Council (Province of British Columbia)

Location: British Columbia, Canada (provincial council; BC residency typically required)

Award: Up to $25,000 CAD per project. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 24 Jun 2026

BC Arts Council's Individual Arts Grants stream for independent media artists working in moving image (film, video, narrative, experimental, expanded cinema, installation), audio/sound art (sound sculptures, installations, sound walks, gallery presentations) or new media and digital arts (interactive installations, immersive environments, web-based art, ICT). ELIGIBILITY: independent individual media artists (not groups or organisations); projects must be independent of commercial industries. As a provincial arts council, BC Arts Council typically requires applicants to be residents of British Columbia - confirm current residency requirements on the linked page before applying. NOTE: registration must be completed by 17 June 2026 at 23:59 PT, ahead of the 24 June application deadline. Applications via the SmartSimple Grant Management System. Contact: Paneet Singh, Program Advisor (Paneet.Singh@gov.bc.ca).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Restless Egg: Luxury Technology Accelerator 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Restless Egg

Location: Remote

Award: $50,000 initial investment for 5% equity, with up to an additional $150,000 follow-on tied to performance milestones. Programme includes weekly check-ins, monthly crits, milestone-driven reviews, access to a network of subject matter experts, collaborators, operators and investors, curated events, and an artist-founder demo day. No application fee.

Duration: Six-month accelerator (programme begins September 2026)

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Six-month Restless Egg accelerator for artist-founders building commercial products with AI and other emerging technologies under the theme of 'Luxury Technology': products that pluralise what AI makes possible across perception, pleasure, communication, ritual and creativity, rather than productivity, surveillance or 'slop'. ELIGIBILITY: artist-founders with an experimental, culturally attuned methodology; work that originates as obsession or frontier practice rather than as a product brief; clearly identified users and real demand; and potential to build a durable company (not a one-off artwork or pure research project). NOTE: this is an equity-based accelerator investment, not a grant - $50,000 initial in exchange for 5% equity, with follow-on conditional on hitting milestones.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Ars Electronica: State of the ART(ist) 2026 (Linz)
item.description:
Organisation: Ars Electronica, in cooperation with the Austrian Federal Ministry for European and International Affairs

Location: Linz, Austria (international open call; project presented at the Ars Electronica Festival)

Award: Main Prize EUR 6,000 plus Award of Distinction EUR 4,000. Both selected projects are presented at the Ars Electronica Festival (9 to 13 September 2026, Linz). No application fee.

Duration: One-off prize plus festival presentation

Deadline: 24 May 2026

International open call by Ars Electronica for artists whose livelihoods and working conditions are severely affected by political repression, war, restrictions on freedom of expression, environmental crises, or economic and systemic exploitation. ELIGIBILITY: individual artists and collectives working in artistic, cultural, social, academic, activist or interdisciplinary fields; proof of risk or instability is a baseline eligibility requirement and projects are then evaluated on artistic quality.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Berggruen Institute: Peking University Research Center Fellowship 2026-2027 (Beijing)
item.description:
Organisation: Berggruen Institute China Center, Peking University

Location: Beijing, China (on-site engagement at PKU expected)

Award: 200,000 CNY annual stipend, paid monthly before tax. Fellows are responsible for their own income tax. No application fee.

Duration: One year, part-time, running 2026 to 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Part-time research fellowship at the Berggruen Institute's Research Center at Peking University, working within one of three core themes (Frontier Science, Technology and Philosophy; Creative Futures; Ancient Wisdom and Planetary Governance), with strong overlap for AI policy, philosophy of technology and computational practice. ELIGIBILITY: open to applicants from any research background; selection is based on the proposal's fit with the Center's themes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Council of Europe: New Democratic Pact for Europe Democracy Hackathon 2026 (Strasbourg)
item.description:
Organisation: Council of Europe (under the Monaco Presidency of the Committee of Ministers), in partnership with No Hate Speech Week and Microsoft

Location: Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, France (hybrid: four online warm-up sessions leading to a three-day in-person finale)

Award: EUR 50,000 Microsoft-funded prize pool distributed among winning teams. Travel and accommodation covered for the 20 selected finalist teams. No application fee.

Duration: Online warm-up sessions plus three-day in-person hackathon (17-19 June 2026)

Deadline: 05 Jun 2026

Council of Europe civic-tech hackathon to combat online hate speech and develop tech solutions for democracy, run alongside No Hate Speech Week and backed by a Microsoft prize pool. Four challenge tracks cover privacy-preserving hate speech detection, identifying coordinated inauthentic behaviour, gender-based violence reporting systems, and innovative social transformation approaches. ELIGIBILITY: pre-formed multidisciplinary teams of 3-4 (typically a technologist/developer, a democracy or policy expert, and a designer or UX innovator); residents of the 46 Council of Europe member states; coding experience is not required for every team member. FORMAT: four online warm-up sessions, then a three-day in-person finale at the Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, 17-19 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Victoria and Albert Museum x Adobe Creative Residency 2026/27
item.description:
Organisation: Victoria and Albert Museum (V&A) and Adobe

Location: V&A South Kensington, London, UK (eligibility restricted to applicants with the right to work in the UK)

Award: 12-month full-time salaried role at the V&A (previous cohort cited at approximately GBP 42,000/year - verify current rate on the V&A site), plus studio, mentorship and access to V&A and Adobe resources. No application fee.

Duration: 12 months full-time

Deadline: 17 Jun 2026

Year-long salaried residency at the Victoria and Albert Museum supported by Adobe, with three thematic tracks for 2026/27 including 'Artificial Intelligence and the Digital World' and 'Making and Meaning - Mixed Media'. Residents take up a full-time employed contract at V&A South Kensington with studio, mentorship and resources from both institutions. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must have the right to work in the UK; open across career stages, with criteria varying by thematic track (see the V&A residency page for track-specific requirements).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Goethe-Institut: Individual Mobility Grants 2026 (Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe)
item.description:
Organisation: Goethe-Institut

Location: Mobility between Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe

Award: Travel costs up to EUR 1,800 (from Europe), accommodation, daily allowances and project expenses covered. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based mobility (variable length)

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

Goethe-Institut individual mobility grants for artists, researchers and curators (including digital and computational practitioners) for research, collaboration, residency placements or project development between Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe. ELIGIBILITY: individual artists, researchers and cultural workers. QUARTERLY DEADLINES: 15 June 2026, 15 September 2026 and 15 December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Sonic Acts: The Walls Have Ears - Sound Studies Writing Residency 2026 (Amsterdam)
item.description:
Organisation: Sonic Acts (with De Ateliers / Woonhuis)

Location: Amsterdam, Netherlands

Award: EUR 3,000 artist fee plus accommodation in Amsterdam, up to EUR 500 EU travel reimbursement, and Sonic Acts Biennial tickets. No application fee.

Duration: One-month writing residency in Amsterdam

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

Funded one-month sound studies writing residency in Amsterdam from Sonic Acts at De Ateliers / Woonhuis. This is a residency for sound-art practitioners and scholars who write about sound (sound studies, criticism, theory) - it is a writing residency, not a sound-production residency. ELIGIBILITY: EU-based artists, researchers and scholars with at least one prior publication in sound studies or sound-art writing.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wonju UNESCO City of Literature / Toji Cultural Centre Residency 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Wonju UNESCO City of Literature and Toji Cultural Foundation

Location: Toji Cultural Centre, Wonju, South Korea (eligibility restricted to writers nominated from a UNESCO City of Literature)

Award: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, meals and local transportation are all covered. NO artist fee or honorarium.

Duration: Eight weeks, 1 September - 31 October 2026

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Eight-week fully funded residency at the Toji Cultural Centre in Wonju, South Korea, hosted by Wonju UNESCO City of Literature. ELIGIBILITY: one writer per cycle, nominated from a UNESCO City of Literature network (Amsterdam, Utrecht, Manchester, Edinburgh, Krakow, Reykjavik, Lillehammer, Iowa City, etc. - see the full UNESCO Creative Cities Network list); 5+ years writing experience required. Open to fiction, nonfiction and poetry. PROCESS: selection by 21 July 2026; residency runs 1 September - 31 October 2026. Apply through your local UNESCO City of Literature coordinator before 31 May 2026. Confirm the exact application route on the Wonju City of Literature site before applying.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Society of Authors: Authors' Foundation and K Blundell Trust Grants (July 2026 round)
item.description:
Organisation: Society of Authors (UK)

Location: UK / international writers contracted with a British publisher

Award: Up to GBP 6,000 per grant. Two streams: Authors' Foundation (open to all writers with a UK-publisher contract) and the K Blundell Trust (for writers under 40 working on socially aware projects). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based, for travel/research/study toward completing a book in progress

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

Biannual works-in-progress grants from the Society of Authors. The Authors' Foundation supports writers contracted with a British publisher across fiction, nonfiction, poetry, drama and scripts. The K Blundell Trust adds a parallel stream specifically for writers under 40 working on socially aware or politically engaged projects. Both schemes share the same two-round-a-year schedule: 1 February and 1 July deadlines. ELIGIBILITY: writers (UK or international) with a contract for the next book with a British publisher; for K Blundell, also under 40 and writing on a contemporary socially aware theme.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: JSK Journalism Fellowship 2027 (John S. Knight Fellowships at Stanford)
item.description:
Organisation: John S. Knight Journalism Fellowships at Stanford

Location: Stanford, California, USA (residential 9-month fellowship)

Award: USD 130,000 stipend over 9 months, plus Stanford tuition, housing, health insurance, and a dependent supplement. No application fee.

Duration: 9 months at Stanford (academic year)

Deadline: 02 Jun 2026

Stanford's flagship journalism fellowship, funded by the John S. Knight Foundation. A nine-month residential, project-based fellowship for working journalists to reimagine the future of journalism - many JSK fellows tackle AI, platforms, information ecosystems, accountability or audience trust. ELIGIBILITY: working journalists with at least 5 years of professional experience; full-time staff, freelancers and entrepreneurial journalists are all eligible; international applicants welcome. PROCESS: application portal opened 6 May 2026; deadline 2 June 2026 at 1pm Pacific. Distinct from the Knight Cities Challenge on the desk - both are Knight-funded but JSK is the Stanford residential fellowship for individual journalists. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Logan Nonfiction Program (Carey Institute) - Fall 2026 and Spring 2027 cycles
item.description:
Organisation: Carey Institute for Global Good (Logan Nonfiction)

Location: Hybrid: residential at the Carey Institute upstate New York, USA, plus virtual engagement

Award: Free residency: lodging, meals and workspace covered. NO cash stipend or honorarium. No application fee.

Duration: Hybrid residency, typically 8-10 weeks per cycle. Fall 2026 cycle runs October-December; Spring 2027 cycle runs February-April.

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

Logan Nonfiction Program is a free hybrid residency for working long-form journalists, writers, podcasters and photojournalists pursuing a long-form non-fiction project (article, book, podcast, film). Two cycles per year: Fall 2026 cycle (residency October-December) deadline 15 June 2026; Spring 2027 cycle (residency February-April) deadline 15 October 2026. The residency is hybrid - residential at the Carey Institute for Global Good in upstate New York combined with virtual engagement. ELIGIBILITY: working journalists, writers, podcasters and photojournalists at any career stage; international applicants welcome.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Nationality for All: SEAP Fellows 2026 (Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific)
item.description:
Organisation: Nationality for All (NFA)

Location: Fully remote; applicants must be early-career human rights professionals based in the Asia Pacific region

Award: AUD 200-400 per month depending on the fellow's location. This is a low-stipend research fellowship; the work itself is full-time over three months.

Duration: 3 months full-time, 15 June - 11 September 2026 (remote)

Deadline: 25 May 2026

Inaugural 2026 SEAP Fellowship Programme from Nationality for All (NFA) for early-career human rights professionals based in the Asia Pacific region. Two full-time, three-month remote fellows will support the updating and verification of the Second Edition of the Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP) - a regional knowledge resource on statelessness, nationality rights, legal identity and exclusion in the Asia-Pacific region. The three sub-regions (Southeast Asia, East Asia, Central Asia) are divided between the two fellows. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: factchecking and additional desk research on existing SEAP country factsheets; updating SEAP website and report content; integrating and verifying citations across SEAP outputs. TIMELINE: Month 1 onboarding and factchecking; Month 2 website and report updates; Month 3 citation integration. ELIGIBILITY: early-career human rights professionals based in the Asia Pacific region with strong desk research, writing and organisational skills; background in human rights, law, public policy, international relations or related disciplines; knowledge of citizenship rights, statelessness and legal identity systems is desirable but not mandatory. Persons with lived experience of statelessness will be prioritised. APPLY: send CV, cover letter and one original writing sample to info@nationalityforall.org with subject line 'Application - SEAP Fellows 2026' by 25 May 2026 (EOD Bangkok time).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Taiwan AID: 2026 NGO Fellowship Program (Asia and Pacific Islands)
item.description:
Organisation: Taiwan Alliance in International Development (Taiwan AID)

Location: Taipei, Taiwan (fully on-site participation required, including a three-week field placement at a Taiwanese NGO)

Award: Fully funded: round-trip airfare, accommodation (dormitory-style rooms, 6-8 beds per room), meals, and local transportation in Taiwan are all covered. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: Four weeks, 4 October - 1 November 2026

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Taiwan Alliance in International Development (Taiwan AID) annual fellowship for NGO professionals from South Asia, Southeast Asia, Northeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. Approximately 12 fellows selected (tentative) for a four-week immersive programme combining (a) a three-week placement at a Taiwanese NGO (event preparation, project management, fundraising activities), (b) workshops on project management, fundraising and advocacy plus civil society site visits in Taiwan, (c) a roundtable with Taiwanese NGO professionals, and (d) attendance at Taiwan LGBT+ Pride. Fellows propose a project draft for future collaboration with their host NGO at the end of the programme and join an alumni network across Asia. ELIGIBILITY: NGO professionals (community development, environment, education, women's rights, civic engagement and human rights, emergency relief, disability inclusion, children and families welfare, social innovation and entrepreneurship; outstanding applicants from other fields also considered); at least three years of NGO sector experience; aged 25-40; from Southeast Asia (Brunei, Cambodia, Laos, Timor-Leste, Indonesia, Malaysia, Myanmar, the Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, Vietnam), South Asia (Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Maldives, Pakistan), Northeast Asia (Japan, South Korea, Mongolia) or Pacific Islands (Palau, Marshall Islands, Tuvalu); proficient in written and oral English (Mandarin a plus).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Flaherty: 2026 Fellowship (71st Flaherty Film Seminar)
item.description:
Organisation: The Flaherty

Location: Vermont, USA (in-person fellows) OR remote (online fellows)

Award: Complimentary Seminar registration for both online and in-person tracks. In-person fellows additionally receive lodging and meals for the duration of the Seminar. The Fellowship does NOT cover travel to and from Vermont. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: Programme runs 7-10 October 2026 (in-person fellows arrive 7 October, depart 11 October); pre-Seminar onboarding in early September; written feedback report due 26 October 2026

Deadline: 09 Jun 2026

The Flaherty's 2026 Fellowship programme for the 71st Flaherty Film Seminar, welcoming 15 in-person fellows in Vermont and 15 online fellows. Of these, 8 in-person and 9 online slots are filled through this open call (the remaining slots are underwritten by academic institutions who select recipients internally). Fellows participate in the full Seminar programme, share their work in Fellows' Screenings and Presentations to Seminar programmers and artists, attend meetings and discussions with featured artists and special guests, and may be asked to facilitate breakout discussions. ELIGIBILITY: active in the field through research, filmmaking or related organisational or project-based work; at least 21 years old; has not received an In-Person Flaherty Seminar Fellowship in the last five years; available for the full duration of the programme. No academic background required. The Flaherty actively encourages applications from BIPOC participants. OBLIGATIONS: pre-Seminar onboarding (virtual) in early September; full attendance through the Seminar 7-10 October 2026; written feedback report due 26 October 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: PEN America: PEN/Heim Translation Fund Grants 2026
item.description:
Organisation: PEN America

Location: Worldwide (translators of any nationality; project must translate a book-length work into English)

Award: USD 4,000 per project (up to 10 awards per year). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based (book-length literary translation in progress)

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

PEN America's annual grants for in-progress book-length literary translations from any language into English. Up to 10 grants of USD 4,000 each. Preference for early-career translators and works from underrepresented languages and regions. Eligible genres include fiction, creative nonfiction, poetry and drama. ELIGIBILITY: translators of any nationality; the project must be a translation of a book-length work into English; previous PEN/Heim recipients are eligible after a waiting period. No application fee. Apply by 15 June 2026 via PEN America's grants portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Nederlands Letterenfonds: Project Grants for Literary Translators (August 2026 round)
item.description:
Organisation: Nederlands Letterenfonds (Dutch Foundation for Literature)

Location: Worldwide (literary translators with a contracted translation from or into Dutch)

Award: Project-based subsidy. Income cap EUR 52,500 for primary income. Standard minimum rates: EUR 0.0802/word for prose; EUR 2.92/line for poetry. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based, scaled to the contracted translation

Deadline: 15 Aug 2026

Project subsidies from the Nederlands Letterenfonds for advanced literary translators with a current publishing contract for a translation from or into Dutch. The next round closes 15 August 2026; decisions are made approximately four months after the deadline (December). ELIGIBILITY: literary translators with a substantial published portfolio and a contracted translation project; primary income must be below EUR 52,500.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Lab: Living Wage Residencies 2026-27 (San Francisco)
item.description:
Organisation: The Lab

Location: San Francisco Bay Area, California, USA (eligibility restricted to artists based in the Bay Area; significant in-person access to The Lab's space in the Mission District is required)

Award: USD 17,530 artist fee per resident (commensurate with three months of work at a San Francisco Bay Area living wage per MIT's Living Wage Calculator) plus USD 5,000 for collaborators and materials. Three months of rehearsal/workshop access to The Lab's space, plus two weeks of continuous use of the exhibition and performance spaces for the residency outcome. Curatorial and marketing support, outside studio visits, documentation and publicity. Stage-2 finalists receive an additional USD 250 stipend to develop their full proposal. No application fee.

Duration: Residencies take place between July 2026 and June 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

The Lab's flagship Living Wage Residency, a substantial in-person residency in San Francisco's Mission District for artists working in any media and discipline. Since 2014 the programme has directed over USD 1 million to artists, with artist fees representing nearly 30% of The Lab's average operating budget. The Lab's history is as a site for experimental and interdisciplinary work spanning performance, exhibition, installation, sound and experimental music, writing, institutional critique, social practice, parades, feasts and further disciplines yet to be defined. The Lab's mission centres traditionally underrepresented artists, particularly those identifying as BIPOC, LGBTQ+, women, disabled and/or from economically marginalised backgrounds. ELIGIBILITY: artists based in the San Francisco Bay Area (tight geographic restriction; in-person access to The Lab's space is central to the residency). SUPPORT per residency: USD 17,530 artist fee (3 months at a Bay Area living wage per MIT's Living Wage Calculator); USD 5,000 for collaborators and materials; three months of rehearsal/workshop access to The Lab's space (as available around other programming); two weeks of continuous use of the exhibition and performance spaces; curatorial and marketing support, outside studio visits, documentation and publicity. PROCESS: Stage 1 application (CV, 3 work links, brief project description and resource plan) due 31 May 2026; Stage 2 invites up to 12 selected artists to develop a full proposal with a USD 250 stipend (Stage 2 deadline TBD). Reviewed by a diverse panel of professional artists and curators plus The Lab's Board. Outside funding additionally secured for a proposal is not factored into the decision, but The Lab may help identify further sources. Supported by the San Francisco Arts Commission.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Video Pool: Video Commission Residency 2026 - VCR Vol.4 (Attrition)
item.description:
Organisation: Video Pool Media Arts Centre

Location: Remote (no in-person attendance required); final showcase at Video Pool's Poolside Gallery in Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada. Equipment/facilities access only for artists based in Winnipeg.

Award: $1,500 artist fee per selected artist (currency not explicitly stated; likely CAD as Video Pool is Winnipeg-based). Artists based in Winnipeg additionally receive access to VP's facilities and equipment (valued at $1,500). Final works added to Video Pool's distribution catalogue and archive.

Duration: Residency period 6 July - 11 September 2026; exhibition September 2026

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

Video Pool Media Arts Centre's 4th Video Commission Residency, VCR Vol.4 'Attrition', commissioning new experimental single-channel video works (5-20 minutes) responding to the theme of attrition: conditions shaped by sustained contact and duration, in images, materials and environments, where form and meaning change through accumulating processes. Artists are encouraged to consider how images and environments are worn down through use, working with analog or digital processes (or both) to explore feedback systems, compression, copying, translation, repeated handling and similar processes that produce difference with each iteration. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists (no nationality restriction stated). No in-person attendance required during the creation process - effectively remote-friendly.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: IT for Change: FemFirst Research Observatory 2026 (Feminist AI Futures in South and Southeast Asia)
item.description:
Organisation: IT for Change

Location: South and Southeast Asia preferred; applicants located in other countries are considered if the research focuses on the region

Award: Approximately CAD 12,000 per proposal for a 12-month period

Duration: 12 months

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

The FemFirst Research Observatory supports mid- to senior-career women and non-binary-identifying researchers and scholars in contributing original research on feminist AI futures in South and Southeast Asia. The Observatory will support 8 to 12 researchers, each developing two high-quality original research papers (6,000 words each) over a 12-month period, alongside two blog syntheses and additional dissemination materials such as podcast episodes. AIMS: build a robust body of empirical, conceptual, policy and applied research at the intersection of AI and gender equality in South and Southeast Asia; open new technical, social and political pathways for feminist AI; and seed an interdisciplinary AI knowledge ecosystem and network of researchers and institutions in the region. ELIGIBILITY: established mid- to senior-career researchers and scholars; may be independent researchers, individual researchers, scholars or academics, or research teams at registered research institutions or entities (universities, research centres, non-profit organisations, for-profit firms). Applicants must demonstrate regional expertise and specialisation in South and Southeast Asia with a track record of work on digitalisation and gender. Applicants must identify as a woman or non-binary person; team applications must be led by a woman or non-binary person. Researchers located outside South or Southeast Asia are considered, but research must focus on the region. Proposed research must be original and not published elsewhere; any additional funding sources for the same proposal must be disclosed.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NEA: Grants for Arts Projects (July 2026 cycle)
item.description:
Organisation: National Endowment for the Arts (NEA)

Location: United States (eligibility for US non-profits with 501(c)(3) status, units of state or local government, or federally-recognised tribal communities)

Award: Project grants typically USD 10,000 to USD 100,000+, with most awards in the USD 10,000-50,000 range; minimum 1:1 cost-share required.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 09 Jul 2026

NEA's flagship project grant for arts organisations across all artistic disciplines. The Grants for Arts Projects (GAP) programme supports projects in dance, design, folk and traditional arts, literary arts, local arts agencies, media arts, museums, music, musical theater, opera, presenting and multidisciplinary works, theater, and visual arts. July 2026 cycle: 'Submit to Grants.gov' deadline 9 July 2026; portal application window 14-21 July 2026. ELIGIBILITY: US non-profits with 501(c)(3) status, units of state or local government, or federally-recognised tribal communities; organisations must have a three-year history of programming and meet a minimum operating budget. Project grants typically USD 10,000 to USD 100,000+, with most awards in the USD 10,000-50,000 range; a 1:1 cost-share is required. Natural sibling to the NEH Public Humanities Projects programme on the desk.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: MATS Autumn 2026 Fellowship (ML Alignment and Theory Scholars)
item.description:
Organisation: MATS Research

Location: Berkeley, California, USA (in-person)

Award: USD 5,000/month stipend plus USD 8,000/month compute budget; housing, meals and travel covered for the 10-week in-person phase. Approximately 80% of fellows receive a 6-12 month extension at USD 7,680/month after the in-person phase.

Duration: Ten weeks in-person, 28 September - 4 December 2026 (with extensions for ~80% of fellows)

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

Fully-funded AI alignment, security and governance fellowship with mentors from Anthropic, Google DeepMind, OpenAI, Redwood Research and other leading AI safety organisations. Fellows work on a focused research project with mentor support during a 10-week in-person programme in Berkeley (28 September - 4 December 2026). New 2026 tracks include Founding/Field-Building and Biosecurity in addition to the core alignment, security and governance tracks. ELIGIBILITY: open to individual researchers, both emerging and experienced; no specific degree or affiliation required - applications are assessed on research aptitude and track record.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds: Activity Programme Grant 2027-2028
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie (Creative Industries Fund NL)

Location: Netherlands

Award: EUR 50,000 to EUR 125,000 per year for the 2027 and 2028 calendar years

Duration: Two years, 2027 and 2028

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

Two-year activity-programme funding from the Creative Industries Fund NL for organisations active in design, digital culture or architecture. Supports a coherent multi-year programme of activities running across 2027 and 2028 (rather than single projects). The funding underwrites a portion of the organisation's annual programme budget, giving recipients planning stability over the two-year period. Application window 13 May - 15 June 2026. EUR 50,000 to EUR 125,000 per year per organisation, depending on the scale and ambition of the proposed programme. ELIGIBILITY: cultural and creative-industries organisations established in the Netherlands working in design, digital culture or architecture; a distinct scheme from the Fund's annual project grants, festival rounds and voucher schemes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stroom Den Haag: PRO Grants 2026 (Research / Projects / Programmes / Investment)
item.description:
Organisation: Stroom Den Haag

Location: The Hague, Netherlands (eligibility restricted to professional visual artists registered with Stroom in the municipality of The Hague)

Award: Four schemes - PRO Research, PRO Projects, PRO Programmes, and PRO Investment - with amounts typically EUR 2,500 to EUR 20,000+ depending on scheme and proposal.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Stroom Den Haag's PRO grants support professional visual artists registered with Stroom in The Hague through four schemes: PRO Research (research and development for new work), PRO Projects (project realisation), PRO Programmes (multi-stage artistic programmes spanning research, production and presentation), and PRO Investment (investment in artistic practice, e.g. equipment, studio, professional development). Three remaining 2026 application rounds: 31 May, 6 September and 25 October 2026. ELIGIBILITY: professional visual artists registered with Stroom in the municipality of The Hague (tightly geographic). Amounts vary by scheme and proposal, typically EUR 2,500 to EUR 20,000+.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Survival and Flourishing Fund (SFF): 2026 Themed Rounds (Climate, Animal Welfare, HSEE)
item.description:
Organisation: Survival and Flourishing Fund (SFF), backed by Jaan Tallinn

Location: Worldwide (grants typically routed through registered non-profits; individual researchers can apply via a fiscal sponsor)

Award: Total USD 20-40M expected across all 2026 SFF rounds. Individual grants typically range from tens of thousands of USD to low millions. Remaining 2026 themed-round deadlines: Climate Theme 10 June; Climate Speculation and Animal Welfare Theme 24 June; Animal Welfare Speculation and HSEE Theme 8 July; HSEE Speculation 22 July (HSEE = Human Self-Empowerment and Existential Risk).

Duration: Project-based; recommended grant lengths vary by round and project

Deadline: 10 Jun 2026

Recommender-grant rounds (the SFF S-Process) funding work that improves the long-term trajectory of life on Earth, backed by Jaan Tallinn and other donors. Funded cause areas span AI safety, biosecurity, climate, animal welfare, and human self-empowerment / existential risk reduction (HSEE). The 2026 cycle includes the closed Main Round (deadline 22 April 2026) plus three themed rounds with paired Theme and Speculation deadlines: Climate (Theme 10 June, Speculation 24 June 2026); Animal Welfare (Theme 24 June, Speculation 8 July 2026); HSEE (Theme 8 July, Speculation 22 July 2026). The S-Process is recommender-driven: recommenders independently evaluate applications and the Fund aggregates their recommendations into grant decisions. ELIGIBILITY: primarily registered non-profit organisations and fiscally-sponsored projects; individual researchers can apply through a fiscal sponsor. Grants typically range from tens of thousands of USD to low millions. Total programme USD 20-40M across all 2026 rounds.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FILE NOT FOUND: Open Call for Archive Residencies
item.description:
Organisation: Goethe-Institut

Location: On-site at ten cultural archives around the world; final exhibition at BLMK Cottbus, Germany

Award: EUR 4,000 (EUR 2,000 artist fee + EUR 2,000 production budget). Travel and accommodation covered up to EUR 2,000 for one person.

Duration: On-site residency of 2 weeks to 2 months, scheduled between August 2026 and May 2027

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

Open call to participate in FILE NOT FOUND, a project exploring the risks and challenges archives face as sites of cultural memory, resistant knowledge and alternative perspectives under growing political pressure. Ten practitioners - artists, thinkers, activists, theatre makers, dancers, musicians and more - will be selected for residencies at ten cultural archives around the world. On site, residents engage with existing archival practices, explore both safe and not-so-safe spaces, investigate traditional and experimental methods of archiving, and ideally develop artistically subversive approaches that strengthen the resilience of archives against external influence. ELIGIBILITY: artists worldwide, all disciplines. Applicants review the ten participating archives and select the one they wish to apply to. The on-site residency runs 2 weeks to 2 months between August 2026 and May 2027. OUTCOME: an artwork plus a residency report, with a group exhibition at BLMK Cottbus, Germany in autumn 2027. Working language English.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Passage Art-Residency-Programme 2026 (KOBRA Cultural Network)
item.description:
Organisation: Polygona Kunstverein e.V. (KOBRA Cultural Network)

Location: Wittenberg region, Saxony-Anhalt, Germany

Award: Per residency place: accommodation for the four-week stay; a grant of EUR 5,000; artists in residence with a child receive an additional EUR 1,000 toward temporary childcare. Travel, local transport, consumables and living expenses are paid by the artist; payment is made on receipt of an invoice.

Duration: Four weeks, October 2026

Deadline: 30 May 2026

Two residency places in the Passage Art-Residency-Programme run by Polygona Kunstverein e.V. as part of the KOBRA Cultural Network, which explores continuities and discontinuities in the cultural landscape between Flaeming and Duebener Heide in the district of Wittenberg (Saxony-Anhalt, Germany). National and international artists working in photography and film are invited to apply. The two 2026 residencies run in October 2026 in collaboration with KOBRA partners Ferropolis Foundation for Industrial Heritage and the Research Library of Reformation Studies Wittenberg (RFB). TOPIC ONE (Ferropolis): artistic perspectives on the history of the former Zschornewitz coal-fired power station, the transformation of industrial landscapes, and visions for the future of post-industrial areas. TOPIC TWO (RFB): exploring the research library as a store of knowledge and examining the production of knowledge and its visual representation, drawing on a collection of around 230,000 volumes including Reformation-era first editions, manuscripts and historical writings. ELIGIBILITY: minimum age 18, no age limit; students are not eligible. Artists are selected by jury on the quality of submitted work; selected artists notified from 20 June 2026. FUNDING per place: accommodation for the four-week stay, a EUR 5,000 grant, and an additional EUR 1,000 for childcare if attending with a child. OBLIGATIONS: four weeks present in October 2026, realisation of an artistic project, provision of ten project photographs by 31 December 2026, delivery of a one-day workshop for young people, a public artist talk, and two days participating in filming for a documentary. Artists retain copyright and ownership of works created. Childcare and child-friendly accommodation can be arranged; partners may also accompany the artist at the artist's own additional cost. No application fee. Apply via the online form at passageair.org by 30 May 2026; applications accepted in German or English.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Art Explora - Academie des beaux-arts European Award 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Art Explora and Academie des beaux-arts

Location: Europe (EU member states, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, the Western Balkans, and Ukraine)

Award: Three award categories based on the organisation's 2025 annual expenditure: Category 1 (up to EUR 200,000 expenditure) - EUR 30,000; Category 2 (EUR 200,000 to EUR 1 million) - EUR 40,000; Category 3 (over EUR 1 million) - EUR 50,000. All applicants automatically become eligible for the Special Jury Award and the Audience Choice Award, each worth EUR 30,000.

Deadline: 26 Jun 2026

Annual award from Art Explora and the Academie des beaux-arts, open to all non-profit European cultural organisations. The European Award encourages new forms of audience engagement and participation in arts and culture, supporting innovative projects across all art forms that can be shared, replicated and scaled across Europe. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be non-profit cultural institutions or organisations based in an EU member state, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, the Western Balkans (Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Republic of North Macedonia, Kosovo, Montenegro, Serbia), or Ukraine (eligible as of 2025). Applicants apply to one of three categories based on their 2025 annual expenditure, with awards of EUR 30,000, EUR 40,000 or EUR 50,000 respectively; all applications are automatically considered for the Special Jury Award and the Audience Choice Award (EUR 30,000 each). Timeline: call open 16 March to 26 June 2026; shortlist committee 4 September 2026; final jury 14 October 2026; award ceremony 24 November 2026. To discuss a project, applicants may write to prix@artexplora.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Creapolis: Malta 2026 - Diversification through Culture
item.description:
Organisation: Creapolis (B Creative Tracks)

Location: Malta (open to Ukrainian professionals currently based in Ukraine)

Award: Fully funded: all travel, accommodation and programme costs are covered.

Duration: One week, 5-9 October 2026

Deadline: 04 Jun 2026

One-week immersive programme in Malta on 'diversification through culture', examining how cultural value is produced, negotiated and redistributed across institutions, public space, tourism, education and community networks. The edition focuses on processes, decision-making structures and systemic dynamics in a dense, interconnected cultural ecosystem, where culture operates simultaneously as heritage, contemporary practice, community expression, public service and economic activity. Programme themes: diversification of cultural practice across heritage, craft, contemporary arts and community traditions; hybrid governance models; culture as a driver of tourism and place-based economies; cultural transmission and identity-building; collaboration between public authorities, civil society and practitioners; adaptive cultural strategies in resource-constrained environments. Participants get hands-on experience, practical tools and collaborative methodologies, exchange with local experts and European peers, exchange with other Ukrainian professionals, and access to a European network for continued cooperation. ELIGIBILITY: Ukrainian professionals currently based in Ukraine, engaged in community and civic development through culture, education, innovation or entrepreneurship - including civil servants and local policymakers, educators, researchers and social innovators, cultural and creative entrepreneurs, and community organisers and local development actors. Applicants should demonstrate clear motivation, relevant experience and a commitment to applying insights to their local Ukrainian context. Working language English. All travel, accommodation and programme costs covered.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: MedSEA: Socially Engaged Arts in the Mediterranean - Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Larnaka 2030, Al Badil, Liv.in.g and Busart (Creative Europe consortium)

Location: Residencies in Larnaka (Cyprus) and Sfax (Tunisia); applicants must legally reside in a Creative Europe-eligible country

Award: Full support: travel, accommodation, artistic fee, mentorship and access to an international network of artists and cultural professionals.

Duration: Multi-phase programme October 2026 - May 2027: capacity-building phase plus a four-week residency (Larnaka 5 October - 1 November 2026; Sfax 5 April - 2 May 2027)

Deadline: 05 Jun 2026

MedSEA (Socially Engaged Arts in the Mediterranean Area) is a Creative Europe cooperation project where artistic practices engage directly with communities, territories and the social transformations shaping the Mediterranean. This open call selects 12 emerging artists and cultural professionals working - or wishing to work - with participatory and socially engaged artistic practices. It is a process of co-creation in real contexts rather than a traditional residency: participants work directly with local communities developing territory-rooted artistic projects. The programme runs in phases - a preparation and capacity-building phase (webinars and workshops on community-based artistic practices, sustainability and cultural project design), then a four-week residency of fieldwork and co-creation supported by project partners. Residencies take place in Larnaka, Cyprus (5 October - 1 November 2026) and Sfax, Tunisia (5 April - 2 May 2027); resulting works are presented at the Mahallart Festival in Larnaka and the Hors-Lits Festival in Sfax. ELIGIBILITY: artists and cultural professionals aged 18 or above, legally residing in a country eligible under the Creative Europe Programme, and actively engaged in community-based or socially engaged artistic practices across disciplines such as visual arts, performing arts, interdisciplinary practices and public space interventions. Selected participants receive full support: travel, accommodation, artistic fee, mentorship and network access. Implemented by a consortium of Larnaka 2030 (Cyprus), Al Badil (Tunisia), Liv.in.g (Italy) and Busart (Greece); co-funded by the Creative Europe Programme of the European Union. Deadline 5 June 2026, 23:59 CET. Contact: maria@larnaka2030.eu.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ifa: Rave Scholarship - Cultural Heritage and Museum Transformation (2027)
item.description:
Organisation: ifa (Institut fuer Auslandsbeziehungen) - Alexander Rave Foundation

Location: Host institutions in Germany (applicants must be cultural practitioners from countries on the OECD DAC list)

Award: Net payment of EUR 1,500 per month; monthly family allowances for accompanying family (EUR 250 spouse, EUR 250 per child); visa costs; outward and return travel costs; health insurance (including family); monthly public transport pass for the city of residence; up to EUR 500 for a language course.

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Scholarship from ifa (Institut fuer Auslandsbeziehungen), supported by the Alexander Rave Foundation, on the management of cultural heritage and the transformation of museums and exhibition centres. It enables cultural practitioners from countries on the OECD DAC list to stay with host institutions in order to deepen their professional knowledge transnationally, introduce new perspectives and build long-term cooperation through exchange. The focus is on transcultural dialogue and engaging with cultural heritage, collections and archive records, funding mutual knowledge transfer and the creation of sustainable networks, and supporting freedom in art and science, freedom of opinion and open debates. Thematic focal areas centre on transforming museums and exhibition venues in line with the ICOM definition of museums (2023): cultural participation, sustainability, non-discriminatory spaces and decolonial working methods. ELIGIBILITY: curators, restorers, mediators and culture managers from DAC-list countries. BENEFITS: net payment of EUR 1,500/month; monthly family allowances (EUR 250 spouse, EUR 250 per child); visa costs; outward and return travel costs; health insurance including family; monthly public transport pass; up to EUR 500 for a language course. Application deadline 30 June 2026 for scholarships beginning after 1 February 2027.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Gasworks Residency Open Call 2026 (Artists based in Greece)
item.description:
Organisation: Gasworks

Location: London, United Kingdom (residency held at Gasworks; applicants must be based in Greece)

Award: Fully funded residency: single-room accommodation in Gasworks' Residency House (shared two-storey house with three communal bathrooms and a kitchen); economy return flights and taxi transfers from the artist's home country to London (one cabin + one hold bag); all visa costs and visa application support; weekly stipend of £175 for living expenses; materials budget up to £800; prepaid TFL zones 1+2 travel card for unlimited central London travel throughout the residency.

Duration: Eleven weeks, 30 September - 15 December 2026

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Eleven-week fully funded residency at Gasworks in London for an artist based in Greece at a pivotal moment in their career. Gasworks' residency programme supports professional development, cultural exchange, and artistic experimentation, hosting up to twenty residencies a year. Residencies are self-led, non-prescriptive and process-based: artists are expected to be self-motivated and to lead on the research and production of their own work, while Gasworks provides administrative, pastoral and curatorial support. Includes 24/7 access to a fully accessible private studio at Gasworks alongside resident artists and London-based studio holders. ELIGIBILITY: artist based in Greece, at a pivotal moment in their career.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Magnetic 5: Franco-UK Artist Residencies (2026 call)
item.description:
Organisation: Fluxus Art Projects

Location: France and United Kingdom (artists from a French region apply to the associated UK residency; artists from a UK nation apply to the associated French region)

Award: Threshold monthly fee of £2,100 / €2,500, plus accommodation, access to a workspace, curatorial mentoring and networking opportunities.

Duration: Two to three months (residency dates set per host)

Deadline: 02 Jun 2026

Magnetic is a joint Franco-UK initiative coordinated by Fluxus Art Projects, bringing together ten institutions for a programme of 2-3 month artist residencies: five in France (Capc, Bordeaux; Frac Grand Large, Dunkirk; Frac Bretagne, Rennes; Villa Arson, Nice; Betonsalon, Paris) and five in the UK (Wysing Arts Centre and Gasworks in England; Flax Art Studios, Northern Ireland; Aberystwyth Arts Centre, Wales; Cove Park, Scotland). Artists from each of the five French regions apply for the associated UK residency; artists from each UK nation apply for the associated French region. Societal and environmental issues are at the heart of the programme; the residencies are situated, with selected artists expected to work in resonance with each host's geographical, historical, societal and artistic context.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Templeton Foundation: Religion, Science & Society - Intelligence (2026 cycle)
item.description:
Organisation: John Templeton Foundation

Location: Worldwide (Templeton funds charitable organizations inside and outside the US)

Award: Two tiers: focused studies under USD $260K; larger collaborative initiatives up to USD $2.5M (using subgrants or internal RFP structures).

Duration: Three years

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Religion, Science & Society (RSS) funding area, 2026 cycle, Intelligence track. In alignment with the 2026 Foundation-wide Intelligence Venture, RSS welcomes three-year proposals applying theological, philosophical, and social-scientific approaches to the guiding themes outlined in the venture description. Two scales of project are supported: small, focused studies under USD $260K, and larger collaborative initiatives up to USD $2.5M that use subgrants or internal Requests For Proposals to structure the work. RSS prioritises interdisciplinary teams. Proposals may include public-engagement components, but the primary activities must be research. Strong fit for projects bringing humanities, social science, and theology into dialogue with AI/cognitive science framings of intelligence. ELIGIBILITY: The Foundation funds charitable organizations only; per Templeton's Apply for a Grant page, 'individuals or applicants from for-profit companies should not apply' (individuals/for-profits are funded only by rare staff invitation). International organizations are eligible; the Foundation has funded organizations from around the world. US applicants are described publicly only as 'charitable organizations' (formal IRS/501(c)(3) verification is handled inside the Templeton Portal after the Funding Inquiry stage); non-US applicants similarly need to be charitable entities, with equivalency handled through the portal. PROCESS: submit an Online Funding Inquiry (OFI) via the Templeton Portal by 15 July 2026; full proposals are by invitation only after OFI review.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Templeton Foundation: Religion, Science & Society - Meaning Making (2026 cycle)
item.description:
Organisation: John Templeton Foundation

Location: Worldwide (Templeton funds charitable organizations inside and outside the US)

Award: Only two project sizes accepted in this track: (1) projects between USD $700K and $1.5M (proposals over $1M should use a Requests For Proposals (RFP) structure to support competitively selected subgrants around a shared theme); (2) focused studies under USD $260K investigating a specific dimension of meaning-making.

Duration: Three years

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Religion, Science & Society (RSS) funding area, 2026 cycle, Meaning Making track. RSS is especially interested in three-year projects that investigate concrete manifestations of meaning making in a world marked by rapid social change, with attention to the practices, experiences, and transitions that enable people to develop, sustain, or renew a life of meaning. STRICT BUDGET WINDOWS: this track will consider ONLY (1) projects between USD $700K and $1.5M (and proposals over $1M must use a Requests For Proposals (RFP) structure with competitively selected subgrants around a shared theme), or (2) focused studies requesting less than USD $260K on a specific dimension of meaning-making. Proposals outside these windows will not be considered. RSS prioritises interdisciplinary teams. Proposals may include public-engagement components, but the primary activities must be research. ELIGIBILITY: The Foundation funds charitable organizations only; per Templeton's Apply for a Grant page, 'individuals or applicants from for-profit companies should not apply' (individuals/for-profits are funded only by rare staff invitation). International organizations are eligible; the Foundation has funded organizations from around the world. US applicants are described publicly only as 'charitable organizations' (formal IRS/501(c)(3) verification is handled inside the Templeton Portal after the Funding Inquiry stage); non-US applicants similarly need to be charitable entities, with equivalency handled through the portal. PROCESS: submit an Online Funding Inquiry (OFI) via the Templeton Portal by 15 July 2026; full proposals are by invitation only after OFI review.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Templeton Foundation: Religion, Science & Society - Imagined Futures (2026 cycle)
item.description:
Organisation: John Templeton Foundation

Location: Worldwide (Templeton funds charitable organizations inside and outside the US)

Award: Priority on exploratory projects with a request amount up to USD $260K.

Duration: Three years

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Religion, Science & Society (RSS) funding area, 2026 cycle, Imagined Futures track. RSS is especially interested in three-year projects exploring how religious, philosophical, and narrative traditions (from theology to science fiction) inform a shared sense of imagination and possibility. In this topical area RSS will prioritise exploratory projects with a request amount of up to USD $260K and clear, achievable outputs. Strong fit for speculative, narrative-driven, or futures-oriented research that bridges humanities, religious studies, and science-fiction studies. RSS prioritises interdisciplinary teams. Proposals may include public-engagement components, but the primary activities must be research. ELIGIBILITY: The Foundation funds charitable organizations only; per Templeton's Apply for a Grant page, 'individuals or applicants from for-profit companies should not apply' (individuals/for-profits are funded only by rare staff invitation). International organizations are eligible; the Foundation has funded organizations from around the world. US applicants are described publicly only as 'charitable organizations' (formal IRS/501(c)(3) verification is handled inside the Templeton Portal after the Funding Inquiry stage); non-US applicants similarly need to be charitable entities, with equivalency handled through the portal. PROCESS: submit an Online Funding Inquiry (OFI) via the Templeton Portal by 15 July 2026; full proposals are by invitation only after OFI review.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Weaving Liberation: Digital Justice Fund (Pilot Round 2026)
item.description:
Organisation: Weaving Liberation (resourced by Ford Foundation, Luminate, and Oak Foundation)

Location: Europe (Council of Europe member states; at least 50% of the group must be based in one or multiple Council of Europe countries)

Award: Grants between €10,000 and €50,000 per group, to be used over up to two years (2026-2028). Total Fund budget for this first round: €500,000.

Duration: Up to two years of project work, between 2026 and 2028

Deadline: 24 May 2026

The Digital Justice Fund works towards nurturing an ecosystem where groups resisting violent technological harms and imagining more nourishing relationships with technologies have the resources they need to dream, organise, build and thrive towards liberatory digital presents and futures. We are interested in funding: Community building and sustaining around digital justice issues: community organising, peer-to-peer support, relationship-building activities, etc. Knowledge and capacity building: popular and political education, investigative journalism, skills exchanges, issue mappings (e.g., technological harms, community needs and desires), advancing liberatory narratives around technology, artistic interventions, and other creative approaches, etc. Coalition and movement building: advocacy, strategic litigation, campaigning, etc. Tech-related mutual aid: tech repair circles, peer support on digital safety and security, shared technical infrastructures for movement work, software/hardware sharing initiatives, etc. Life-affirming technology work: prototyping, piloting, or expanding community-centred, transfeminist, anti-colonial technologies, etc. Key dates: platform registration deadline Sunday 24 May 2026, 23:59 CET (registration is required by this date even if application is not yet complete); final application deadline Sunday 21 June 2026, 23:59 CET.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BrightBrain: Artist Residency 2026 (TU Braunschweig Science & Art Lab)
item.description:
Organisation: Science and Art Lab at TU Braunschweig, in cooperation with the BrightBrain research consortium

Location: Braunschweig, Germany (in-person residency at TU Braunschweig)

Award: Artist fee €2,400 per month (gross), plus travel costs and accommodation covered, plus production budget up to €3,500 (gross).

Duration: Autumn/winter 2026 residency (start from September 2026, exact start date flexible). Length agreed with host.

Deadline: 25 May 2026

Open call for an international artist-in-residence at the Science and Art Lab at TU Braunschweig, in cooperation with the BrightBrain research consortium, as part of the Ecoversity initiative. The residency aims to connect artistic and scientific perspectives. Artists are invited to propose their own research-based projects related to microphotonics, biomedicine, technology, neurodegenerative diseases, neurobiology, neuromorphic computing, and microelectronics (the current resident is collaborating with the Nitride Technology Center on light transmission in digital applications including micro-LEDs and neuromorphic computing). Strong fit for art-science, sensory translation, critical-data, and neuroscience-informed practices. Eligibility: international artists; should show interest in biomedicine, technology and innovation in their artistic work; be comfortable working in interdisciplinary environments; be motivated to collaborate with experts from science, culture and education; be willing to participate in two public events during the residency; and be able to communicate in English. Application by PDF to artist-residency@tu-braunschweig.de with subject 'BrightBrain residency' (if files exceed 25 MB, upload via the TU Braunschweig Nextcloud link in the call). Application deadline 25 May 2026. Jury decision expected end of June 2026; residency starts from September 2026 (exact date flexible). Free to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Banff Centre: Visual Arts Thematic Residency - Future Figurations (2027)
item.description:
Organisation: Banff Centre for Arts and Creativity

Location: Banff, Alberta, Canada (in-person residency on the Banff Centre campus in the Canadian Rockies)

Award: Paid program. Total before scholarships: CAD $9,484.55 per person. With the Banff Centre Arts Scholarship (100% tuition + 25% accommodation/meals): CAD $4,384.73 per person. Canadian Indigenous Scholarship: 100% tuition + accommodation + meals (no out-of-pocket). Application fee: CAD $65 (individuals/groups), CAD $35 for Indigenous applicants. No artist stipend.

Duration: 5 weeks total (arrival 24 January 2027, program 25 January-26 February 2027, departure 27 February 2027)

Deadline: 03 Jun 2026

Visual Arts Thematic Residency at Banff Centre led by artist-faculty Rajni Perera and Marigold Santos. Drawing on ideas of speculative futures, science fiction, myth-making, and world-building, artists are invited to research, imagine and create possible selves and possible worlds. The program considers futurity as an anti-oppressive and restorative force, exploring new possibilities of selfhood and agency by decolonising ideas of the body. Open to international applicants aged 18+ with post-secondary formal training or equivalent experience, working in visual arts disciplines: ceramics, digital media, painting, drawing, printmaking, papermaking, photography, sculpture, installation.
